Short title and commencement.- (l) These rules may be called the Works of Licensees Rules, 2006. Definitions.- (l) Il these mles unless the context otherwise requires,- (a) "The Act" means the Eleckicity Act. 200-1: (b) "occupier" of any building or land means a person in la*firl occupation ofthat building or land. (2) All other words and expression used herein and not defined in these rules, shall hal'e the meanings rcspectively assigned to them in the Act. 3. Licensee to uLrry out works.- (l) A licensee may- (a) carry out wo*s, lay down or place any electric supply line or other works in, through, or against, any building, or on, over or under any land whereon, *trereover or whereunder any electric supply-line or works has not already been lawfully laid down or placed by such licensee, with the prior consent of the owner or occupier ofany building or land; (b) fix any support ofoverhead line or any stay or strut required for the purpose of sccuring in position any support of an overhead line on any building or land or having been so fixed, may aher such support: Provided that in case ttrere the owner or occupier of the building or land raises objections in respect of works 1o be carried out under this rule, the licensee shall obtain permission in writing from the District Magistate or the Commissioner of Police or any other officer authorised by the State Govemme in this behalf, for carrying out the *orks: .l ]'rovided frrrther that if at any time. the owncr or occupier of any building or land on which any works have becn carried out or any support of an overhea'd line, stay or stfut has been fixed shows suffrcient cause, the Dislrict Magistrate or the Commissioner of Police, or the officer authorised may by order iII i.iting direct for any such works, support, stay or strut to be removed or altered. (2) When making an order under suFrule (l). the District Magistrale or the ilommissioner of Potic.-e or the oticer so authorised, as the case may be, shall fix ' after considering the representations of the concemed persons ,if any' the amount "i-."ip.*",1"" o, oi annual rent, or of both. which should in his opinibn bc paid bl the licensee to thc owner or occupier' lt I (2) They shall come into force on the date oftheir publication in the Official Gazelle. 2.. -- 11 of 17 -- l) TllE GAZEIn oI: INDIA : EXIIAORDII,IAIiY JP^(r (3) Evcry order made by a District Magislrale or a Commissioner of Police or an authorised olficer under sub-rule (1) shall be subject to revision by the Appropriate Commission. (4) Nothing contained in this rule shall el-i'ecl the ptuers confened upon anv licensee under section 164 of the Act. 4. (6) Where the works to be executed consist of the laying of any underground service line immedialely attached, or intended to be immediately atlached, to a distributing main, the licensee shall give Io the repairing authority or the works authorily, as the case may be, not less than forty-€ight hours' notice in writing of his intention to execute such works; (7) Where the works to be executed consist of the repair, rcnewal or arnendment of existing wo*s ofwhich the character or position is not to be altered, the licensee shall, except in cases of emergency, give to the repairing authority, or to the works authority. as the case.may be. not less than forty-eight hours' notice in writing of his intertion to execute such works, and, on the expiry of such notice; such works shall be commenced forthwith and shall be carried on with all reasonable despatch. and, if possible. both by day and by night until completed. 5. Repairs and works during emergency'- The licensee may' in case -of emergency due to the breakdown of an underground electric supplyJine' after infor;ing the repairing authority or the works authority' as the case may be' of his intention to do so, place an overhead line without complying wilh the provisions ofrule 4 : Provided that srch overhead line shall be used only until the defect in the underground electric supplyJine can be made good, and in no case (unless *iL tir. irii.r.onsent of the repairing authorili, workfauthority or occupier as the case may be) for a pcriod excteding six weeks, and shall be removed a^s soon as may be after such defecl is removed' 6. (2) Where thc operator finds it necessary to undermine, but not to alter. the position of any pipe, electric supplyJine or u.ork, he shall support it in position during the execution of the rvork, and befbre completion shall provide a suitable and proper foundation for it vv'here so undermined. (3) Where the operator (being the licensee) lavs any electric supply-line across. or so as to be liable to touch, any pipes. lines or sen icc-pipes or sewice-lines belonging to any duly authorised person or to any person supplying. transmitting or using energy under the Act. he shall not, except with the written consent of such person and in accordanee rvith the regulations on safety as specified under section 53 oithe Aet. lay his electric supply-iines so a*s to come into contact with any such pips. lines or service-pipes or service-lines. Alteration of the position of pipes, electric 1ine. etc.-(1) Any licensee may alter the position of any pipe (nor fonr,ing part of a local authority s main sewer), oI of any rvire under 'or oYer any place rvhich he is authorised to open or break up. if such pipe or wrre is Iikely to intcdere with the exercise of his porvers under the Act; and any persor may alter lhc position of an.v electnc supply-lincs or r'vorks of a licensee undet ot over an.v such place as aforesaid, if such electric supply-lines or wotks arc likcl-v to interfcre with the la*firl exgrcise of anv po\rers vested in him. Works not repairable by the Appropriate Govemment, licensee or local authorit-v.- Jhe licensee shall open or break up any street not repairable by the Central Government or the Slate Govemment or a local authority only rvith the written consent of the person by whom lhe streel is repairable or mth the written consent of the Appropriate Government: Provided that the Appropriate Government shall not give any such consent as aforesaid, until the licensee has given notice by advertisement or otherwise as that Governmenr may rlirect. and u"ithin such period as the thar Govemment may fix in this behall to the person above refened to, and until all represenutions or objcctions received in accordance with the notice have been considered bv the that Govemment. (l) Where any person. in exercise of any of thr: po*ers conferred b1; or under these rules opens or breaks up the soil or pavcment ol any street, rail*ay or tmmway, or any sewer, drain or tunnel. he shall- (a) immediately cause fte part opened or broken up to be fenced and guaded and fix caution boards to alert trafficr (b) cause a light or lights, suticient for the waming of passengers before sunset, to be set up and maintained until sumise against or near the part opened or broken up; (c) make suitable arralgements for smooth 1'lo*' of traffic; (d) frll in the ground and reinstate and makc good the soil or pavement, or the server. drain or hrnnel. opened or broken up with all reasonable speed, and carr,v away the rubbish occasioned by such opening or breaking up: and (e) after r0instaling and making good the siil or pavement. or the seraer. drain or tunnel broken or opened up. keep the same in good repair for three months and tbr any funher period. not exceeding uine months, during which subsidence c0ntinues. Manner of deposit of amount for restoration of railways, hamways, waterways etc.- The licensee shall deposit the amount for restoration of railways. tramways, wat€rways e(c. under these rules by means of demand draft in favour of the officer-in-charge of the maintenance ofthe works concerned. 12. Manner of restoration of propertl' affected by such works and maintenance thereof.- The licensee shall carry out the restoration of property aflected by worlis and undertake necessary maintenance thereof for one month. 13. Determination and payment of compensation to affected persons,- (l) Where the liccnsee makes default in complying with any of ttre provisions of these rules, he shall make full mmpensation for any loss or damage incurred by reason thereof to the person affected, as may be determined by the District Magistrate or by any other officer authorised by the State Govemment in this behalf.ifnot agreed mutually between the parties concerned. (2) Where any di{ference and dispute arises as to the amount of compensation determined under sub-rule (l), the malter shall be determined by the Appropriate Commission. 14. Procedure for deposit of compensation payable by the licensee and furnishing ofsecurity.- (l) The amount ofcompensation payable by thc licensee under these rules shall bc dcposiled by means of demand draft. (2) The security required to be fumished under these rules shall be in the form of Banl< Guarantee from a Scheduled Banl or in any other form as may be notified by the Appropriate Govemment fiom time to time. 15. Determination of dispute or difference by the Apfropriate Commission '- When a matter is brought 1o the Appropriate Commission for determinalion under these rules, the matter shall be determined by the Appropriate Commission within a period of thirty days and after hearing the parties concerned. 16. Scrvice of notice etc.- Whenever a nolic€ or intimation is required to be served upon a person under these rules, the procedure provided under section l7l ofthe Act and rules made thereunder shall be followed.
